summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, AI / computing, Democracy / anti-fascism. The strongest items in this run are Framework announces Laptop 13 Pro, ‘the MacBook Pro for Linux users’, Southern Poverty Law Center indicted on federal fraud charges, and Texas can require public schools to display Ten Commandments, court rules.

mainItems.title: Framework announces Laptop 13 Pro, ‘the MacBook Pro for Linux users’
mainItems.summary: Every time we review a Framework laptop, we find familiar pros and cons. They're truly upgradable, incredibly repairable, but we always wish the battery lasted longer. We always wish the build quality were top notch.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Southern Poverty Law Center indicted on federal fraud charges
mainItems.summary: Todd Blanche announces 11-count indictment over payments to informants in extremist groups including Ku Klux Klan Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The Southern Poverty Law Center, the prominent…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Texas can require public schools to display Ten Commandments, court rules
mainItems.summary: Appeals court ruling is a victory for conservatives who have long sought to incorporate more religion into schools Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Texas can require the Ten Commandments to be…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: Trump reads from Bible in Oval Office in taped message for Christian group
mainItems.summary: Reciting of Old Testament passage comes days after clash with pope and posting AI image of himself as Jesus Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Donald Trump read a Bible passage from the Old…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Internal emails show how Amazon raises prices across the Internet, lawsuit says
mainItems.summary: “It’s working!“: Amazon emails with vendors show pattern of disappearing deals.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: Arcade roguelike KAZ will probably break my arrows keys, and yet here I am wanting to play another round
mainItems.summary: The less buttons I have to press a game, the better. I am not doing a bit here, no japes to be found, a refined, paired down control scheme is like a cherub's smile to me.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Framework's CEO on the RAM crisis and creating a "MacBook Pro for Linux users"
mainItems.summary: "We actually have slightly more Linux users than Windows users."
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to humane computing, open-source practice, and repairability.

mainItems.title: Trump's order is a milestone for proponents of using psychedelics as medicine
mainItems.summary: President Trump has signed an executive order aimed at accelerating research and approval of psychedelics for treating mental health conditions, like depression, PTSD and addiction.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: Opinion: School Vouchers Fail the Civil Rights Test. The Federal Program Is No Exception
mainItems.summary: Our public education system rests on a foundation of civil rights protections. Public schools exist to serve every child.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: California Schools Face Budget Cuts as Enrollment Drops by 74,961 Students
mainItems.summary: Enrollment in California K-12 schools, and in schools across the country, is declining rapidly as birth rates drop and immigration rates fall.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: This AI Tool Rips Off Open Source Software Without Violating Copyright
mainItems.summary: Malus, which is a piece of satire but also fully functional, performs a "clean room" clone of open source software, meaning users could then sell software without crediting the original developers.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: What having a product guy as Apple's CEO might mean for the company
mainItems.summary: Apple's next CEO has been responsible for developing the hardware for many products. NPR's Juana Summers talks to Jay Peters of The Verge about what the company could look like under his leadership.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Interesting where engineering, science, and tech infrastructure collide.

mainItems.title: Some Silo Secrets May Finally Be Explained When Season 3 Premieres in July
mainItems.summary: We do not know why we are here. We do not know who built the silo. But we might, soon! The post Some Silo Secrets May Finally Be Explained When Season 3 Premieres in July appeared first on Reactor .
mainItems.whyItMatters: On-theme for your cultural interests, especially when the concept is strong.

highlights.title: A.I. Is Prompting an Evolution, Not Extinction, for Coders
highlights.notes: Interesting piece on the current state of AI in software work.

highlights.title: The Workers Letting A.I. Do Their Jobs
highlights.notes: In an era of agents powered by artificial intelligence, many programmers are barely programming.

highlights.title: Trump’s presidency is what evil looks like: absurd, frightening, cruel
highlights.notes: Particularly strong Guardian opinion piece, noted for future briefing highlights.
